Off Calendar Nov-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Christian E. Baker; Manatt, Phelps & Phillips, LLP; 415-291-7400; appearing on behalf of Cocoa AJ Holdings, LLC, Rick Houston, Kevin Morgan, and Todd Chapman.

No other appearances. Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. As to the second cause of action the demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer should be sustained with leave to amend to allege facts supporting the conclusory allegation regarding damages; demurrer should be otherwise overruled. As to the third cause of action demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions regarding damages. As to the fourth cause of action demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege an actionable cause of action under Subdivided Lands Act in good faith stating facts in support of each element of this cause of action including relief. The Court notes that Plaintiff cited no cases (other than discussing authorities cited by the Defendant) in its opposition pertaining to availably of a claim under the Subdivided Lands Act and expects the Plaintiff to only assert this cause of action in an amended complaint in good faith. It appears from the opposition that Plaintiff may want to assert a breach of CC&Rs claim arising out of the violation of the Subdivided Lands Act. Plaintiff may do so in the amended complaint if such allegations may be done in good faith. As to the fifth cause of action demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts showing that Plaintiff has already lost money or property. As to the sixth cause of action demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions to support each element of this cause of action.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
